title: Russia's new homegrown Soyuz 5 rocket aces debut launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz 5 medium-lift rocket for the first time on April 30. The debut flight was reported to have gone smoothly.

title: Artemis 3 has been pushed to late 2027. Can NASA still land astronauts on the moon in 2028?
summary: Artemis 3 has been delayed to late 2027 due to issues with Starship and Blue Moon lander development. This delay puts NASA's planned 2028 moon landing at risk.

title: US Space Force wants space-based missile interceptors for Golden Dome ready by 2028
summary: The US Space Force is developing space-based missile interceptors under a new program aiming for a demonstration by 2028. The initiative, dubbed 'Golden Dome,' seeks to enhance missile defense capabilities.

title: Is Venus volcanically active? Big Hawaiian eruption in 2022 could help scientists find out
summary: Evidence suggests Venus may still be volcanically active, and studying a major 2022 eruption in Hawaii could help confirm this. Scientists believe new data from the Hawaiian event could provide insights into Venusian volcanic processes.

title: Meet the legendary heroes and villains of 'Masters of the Universe' (video)
summary: A video titled 'Masters of the Universe' highlights legendary heroes and villains from the franchise. The creators emphasize their commitment to honoring fan expectations in visualizing the world.

title: Astrophotographer captures Pleiades 'Seven Sisters' glowing through ghostly blue veil
summary: An astrophotographer captured a stunning image of the Pleiades star cluster, also known as the 'Seven Sisters,' shining through wispy blue nebula clouds. The photo showcases the blue-white light of the stars reflecting off the surrounding nebula.

title: This SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket launch looks amazing from space in these wild satellite photos
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy rocket launched after an 18-month hiatus, and a satellite captured stunning images of the event from space.

title: Welcome home! Artemis 2's Orion capsule returns to Florida after epic moon mission (photo)
summary: Artemis 2's Orion capsule has returned to Florida after completing a historic mission around the moon's far side with four astronauts. The spacecraft arrived at its launch site just three weeks after the epic journey.

title: An Amazon rainforest river from space | Space photo of the day for April 30, 2026
summary: A NASA astronaut captured a space photo of the Ucayali River snaking through the Amazon rainforest from the International Space Station. The image was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for April 30, 2026.

title: 'This is the Way': Lego goes big with 9 new sets for this year's May the 4th Star Wars drop, including the first Star Wars UCS set of 2026
summary: Lego announced nine new Star Wars-themed sets for the 2026 May the 4th event, including the first Star Wars UCS set. The collection features the Mandalorian’s N-1 Starfighter, which will appear in the upcoming Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu movie.

title: NASA Invites Media to Ireland Artemis Accords Signing
summary: Ireland is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington on May 4. The event will be hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and attended by Irish officials.

title: Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars
summary: NASA's 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter mission celebrated its 25th anniversary on April 15, 2026, with a gathering of team members. The event featured a giant global map of Mars created using imagery from Odyssey’s THEMIS instrument.

title: What’s Up: May 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA provides skywatching tips for May 2026, including meteor showers, a lunar conjunction with Venus, and a rare blue moon at month's end.

title: Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage on the Move
summary: The core stage of NASA's SLS rocket for the Artemis III mission was moved into the Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center. The stage traveled 900 miles from NASA's Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans via the Pegasus barge.

title: NASA Welcomes Morocco as 64th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Morocco signed the Artemis Accords on April 29th in Rabat, becoming the 64th signatory and committing to responsible space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Morocco's participation during the ceremony.

title: NASA Goddard’s Greenbelt Visitor Center Marks 50th Anniversary
summary: NASA Goddard's Greenbelt Visitor Center celebrates its 50th anniversary, having opened in May 1976 to inspire through exhibits and programs on space exploration. The center continues to fulfill its mission of sharing NASA's work with the public.

title: NASA Explores Prioritizing First Response Drones in Crowded Skies
summary: NASA is working to prioritize first response drones in crowded skies to ensure they can operate effectively alongside other commercial drones. The initiative aims to manage air traffic as drone deliveries and services become more common.

title: Winter’s End Is Written in the Clouds
summary: The transition from winter to spring over the Gulf of Alaska showcased various cloud formations. This meteorological event highlights natural atmospheric patterns.

title: I Am Artemis: Ryan Schulte
summary: Ryan Schulte, Orion flywheel project manager, discusses the Artemis II astronauts' 694,481-mile journey around the Moon, highlighting the Orion spacecraft's life support and the crew's use of a flywheel exercise device for health maintenance.

title: US-Indian Spacecraft Captures Mexico City Subsidence
summary: A scientist used NISAR mission data to create a map showing land subsidence in Mexico City. The NISAR mission, a collaboration between NASA and ISRO, is demonstrating its capabilities in tracking geological changes.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket on Saturday. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut has experience in mass casualty events and overseas service.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return to flight for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent Artemis II mission, which successfully flew four astronauts by the Moon.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is making a return, while Atlas V and Ariane 6 are also scheduled for launches.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the recent CRS NG-24 mission.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on Starship at Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year as the Block 3 variant nears its first flight.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from the NASA Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the program's timeline and potential impacts on future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: A busy week in space launches is expected with seven missions planned across four countries using five different launch vehicles. Key missions include GPS, Progress, and Starlink deployments.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark milestones. The publication reflects on the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology over five decades.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or deployment was conducted. The specific details are not provided in the given text.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination, as part of planetary protection measures. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing space debris or satellites in orbit. This innovation could significantly improve space debris management and satellite servicing operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback from smaller enterprises on space sector challenges.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the satellite. The mission highlights advancements in space-based navigation technology.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has generated 57 artificial solar eclipses and collected over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data since July 2025. The mission revealed that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has unveiled a new satellite product designed for urban planning and monitoring. The tool aims to provide construction firms, insurers, and city authorities with real-time data on urban changes and risks.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance multi-domain capabilities.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The competitive landscape for orbital data centers includes major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, Sophia Space, Starcloud, Aetherflux, and Axiom, along with satellite manufacturing suppliers such as Apex and K2 Space.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit AI processing by running Liquid AI's large language model on a satellite, analyzing an Earth observation image without ground transmission. The mission, Clustergate-2, was launched aboard Momentus' Vigoride 7 satellite in March.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon stated that the Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, implying no unique differentiators compared to existing commercial satellite buses.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump over proposed cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it harmful to science and innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Isaacman Continues to Defend Budget Cuts to Congress
summary: Isaacman defended budget cuts to Congress while emphasizing NASA's goal to accelerate the US return to the Moon. The hearing highlighted the agency's top priority in space exploration.

title: True Anomaly Closes $650M Series D
summary: True Anomaly, a four-year-old space company, raised $650 million in a Series D funding round. The funding follows the company's recent Golden Dome award for its space-based interceptor technology.

title: Meta Secures Overview Energy Space Solar Power Capacity
summary: Meta has secured up to 1 GW of power capacity through Overview Energy's planned solar power-beaming satellite system. This agreement marks a significant step in leveraging space-based solar power for large-scale energy needs.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI Tech program, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion in total. This initiative aims to advance space-based defense technologies.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discusses tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions at Modern Day Marine. The conversation highlights how Lite Coms designs products for network resiliency.

title: Skynopy Signs Frequency Management Partnership with CNES
summary: Skynopy has partnered with CNES to optimize frequency allocation for its ground stations in France. This collaboration aims to enhance frequency management for Skynopy's operations.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Terminal Developer All.Space in $355M Deal
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring terminal developer All.Space in a $355 million deal to create a complete communications ecosystem. The acquisition aims to strengthen York's position in the space communications sector.

title: FCC Updates EPFD Framework that Covers GEO/NGSO Spectrum Sharing
summary: The FCC adopted a new framework for Equivalent Power Flux Density (EPFD) to govern spectrum-sharing between GEO and NGSO systems. This update addresses longstanding concerns about interference and coordination in satellite communications.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing to launch six satellites this quarter, marking a significant milestone as it transitions to commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed the upcoming launch in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) announced an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy. This move aims to enhance technological capabilities in the space sector.

title: Canada Cancels WildFireSat Constellation Contract With Spire
summary: Canada has canceled its contract with Spire Global to build the WildFireSat constellation of wildfire-monitoring satellites. The government still intends to proceed with the space-based wildfire monitoring program.

title: SpaceX Launches Final ViaSat-3 Satellite, Completing Trio of GEO Satellites
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the final ViaSat-3 satellite, completing Viasat's trio of high-capacity Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites using a Falcon Heavy rocket. This mission marks the end of a significant satellite deployment for Viasat.

title: Orbcomm Completes $460M Refinancing to Fund Growth
summary: Orbcomm has completed a $460 million refinancing to support growth initiatives and large-scale customer deployments. The funding will expand the company's platform.

title: UPCOMING: Designing Secure Satellite Systems with FIPS 140-3, CNSA 2.0, and PQC
summary: Designing secure satellite systems is becoming challenging due to evolving security requirements like CNSA 2.0 and FIPS 140-3, especially for long-term deployments. The article discusses upcoming solutions to address these security needs.
